title: Shenzhen Science Multimodal Foundation Model Makes Debut in Shanghai: 11 Billion Parameters Integrate Six Types of Scientific Data, One Model Understands DNA to Weather Fields
excerpt: On July 19, during the Scientific Intelligence Open Forum at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference, the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligen
content_html:
On July 19, during the Scientific Intelligence Open Forum at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference, the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence unveiled a foundational super brain - Shenzhen Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model, which supports multidisciplinary scientific intelligence research. The name is derived from the "Divine Pearl Iron" in Journey to the West. The team hopes that this open model can carry out diverse scientific tasks in a relatively concise form and allow more researchers to participate in testing, using, and co-building it. It is the "Great Sage" system-level intelligent research agent, launched in early March of this year, with its super brain.

Technically, Shenzhen is based on multiple disciplines such as material science, life science, and earth science, and has achieved a stage-wise unified representation of cross-domain scientific knowledge and multimodal integrated understanding. The model has about 11 billion parameters and supports scientific understanding and multi-type result generation for six types of scientific data: DNA, RNA, proteins, small molecules, Earth systems, and medical images, all within a single model. Its shared backbone uses Qwen3-VL-8B, and each of the six types of scientific data has a dedicated data processing path. Before connecting to the shared model, it preserves the sequence order of sequences, molecular connection structures, meteorological field spatial distribution, and local details of medical images. More practically, Shenzhen can directly generate RNA sequences, computer-readable molecular representations (SMILES), global meteorological fields, and medical image segmentation results, effectively integrating both understanding and generation capabilities into one framework.

Open source and openness are key characteristics of this model. The Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence has released model weights, inference code, example scripts, and usage documentation. Researchers can download the model or call APIs through the Xinghe Qizhi Scientific Intelligence Open Platform, and use them together with over 1,500 scientific models and tools available on the platform; they can also download weights on Hugging Face and get code on GitHub, jointly building an open scientific intelligence community.

This release took place on the stage of the Scientific Intelligence Open Forum at WAIC2026. The forum was guided by the Office of the World Artificial Intelligence Conference Organizing Committee, and hosted by Fudan University and the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence. It focused on the scientific closed-loop discovery in the AI-native era, gathering many renowned scientists and young representatives from industry, academia, and research, deeply discussing how artificial intelligence could restructure the entire process of scientific discovery from the methodology level, aiming to create a new ecological environment of open and collaborative scientific intelligence. Four distinguished scientists delivered keynote speeches, opening up different dimensions to understand scientific intelligence.

Professor Arie Y. K. Wachter, winner of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry and professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen, emphasized that AI must be built on reliable physical mechanisms. Professor Gilles Brassard, winner of the Turing Award and professor at the University of Montreal, expressed high expectations for the potential of quantum computing in fields like new material discovery, and advised young researchers to follow their interests rather than industry trends, as current research findings might only truly take effect after a decade.

Director of the Zhejiang Lab and academician of the Chinese Academy of Engineering, Wang Jian, shared his views on scientific foundation models and science as a whole. He pointed out that scientific intelligence lies not in paper texts but in data, yet today's foundation models are still based on text. In the future, scientific data should become the native inhabitants of scientific intelligence, meaning that data from various disciplines should be tokenized and included in a common representation space. He believes AI is becoming as fundamental as mathematics, and the research paradigm will shift from STEM to STE+MAP, that is, the integration of mathematics, AI, and public infrastructure, making science a whole again.

Professor Jianqing Fan, member of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences and professor at Princeton University, spoke on the topic of intelligent science and society, summarizing AI as a dynamic cycle of statistical learning and optimization decision-making, and explained how AI transforms data into social insights with examples such as socioeconomic measurement, financial risk control, and large model applications. He emphasized that AI serves socioeconomic development, while promoting intelligent agents and scientific innovation, it must continuously respond to issues such as changes in employment structure, educational transformation, ethical security, and whether humans can maintain effective control.

In the summit dialogue session, participants reached a consensus: a major key to original innovation in the AI-native era lies in whether people, data, models, experiments, and academic judgment can form a new collaborative discovery mechanism. This requires not only systematic upgrades in research infrastructure, but also a fundamental transformation in the thinking patterns and capability structures of a generation of researchers.

Qi Yuan, a specially appointed professor at Fudan University and director of the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence, outlined the ultimate vision of this transformation. He stated that AI should move from predicting the next token to discovering unknown laws, and that the discovery of unknown scientific laws by AI will mark the beginning of super intelligence. The core of this process lies in compressing high-dimensional space to discover concise principles and achieving an efficient scientific verification loop, which will promote the integration of digital and physical worlds and the emergence of new model architectures. He reminded us that real research involves a long chain process including literature, hypotheses, data, models, simulations, experiments, and feedback, and the field of scientific intelligence is currently focusing on developing the systemic capabilities to organize complex research processes.

When Shenzhen, with its 11 billion parameters, brings six types of scientific data into one mind, the form of scientific discovery may be quietly rewritten.

sentences.zh: 7月19日，在2026世界人工智能大会科学智能开放论坛上，上海科学智能研究院发布了支撑多学科科学智能研究的基础超级大脑——深圳科学多模态基础模型。

sentences.zh: 名字来源于《西游记》中的“神珠铁”。

sentences.en: The team hopes that this open model can carry out diverse scientific tasks in a relatively concise form and allow more researchers to participate in testing, using, and co-building it.

sentences.en: It is the "Great Sage" system-level intelligent research agent, launched in early March of this year, with its super brain.
sentences.zh: 它是今年3月初推出的“大圣”系统级智能研究代理，拥有超级大脑。

blocks.html_en: Technically, Shenzhen is based on multiple disciplines such as material science, life science, and earth science, and has achieved a stage-wise unified representation of cross-domain scientific knowledge and multimodal integrated understanding. The model has about 11 billion parameters and supports scientific understanding and multi-type result generation for six types of scientific data: DNA, RNA, proteins, small molecules, Earth systems, and medical images, all within a single model. sentences.en: Technically, Shenzhen is based on multiple disciplines such as material science, life science, and earth science, and has achieved a stage-wise unified representation of cross-domain scientific knowledge and multimodal integrated understanding.
sentences.zh: 技术上，深圳以材料科学、生命科学、地球科学等多学科为基础，实现了跨领域科学知识和多模态综合理解的阶段性统一表征。

sentences.en: The model has about 11 billion parameters and supports scientific understanding and multi-type result generation for six types of scientific data: DNA, RNA, proteins, small molecules, Earth systems, and medical images, all within a single model.
sentences.zh: 该模型拥有约 110 亿个参数，支持对六种类型的科学数据进行科学理解和多类型结果生成：DNA、RNA、蛋白质、小分子、地球系统和医学图像，所有这些都在一个模型中。

sentences.en: Its shared backbone uses Qwen3-VL-8B, and each of the six types of scientific data has a dedicated data processing path.
sentences.zh: 其共享主干采用Qwen3-VL-8B，六类科学数据中的每一种都有专用的数据处理路径。

sentences.en: Before connecting to the shared model, it preserves the sequence order of sequences, molecular connection structures, meteorological field spatial distribution, and local details of medical images.
sentences.zh: 在连接到共享模型之前，它保留了序列的序列顺序、分子连接结构、气象场空间分布以及医学图像的局部细节。

sentences.en: More practically, Shenzhen can directly generate RNA sequences, computer-readable molecular representations (SMILES), global meteorological fields, and medical image segmentation results, effectively integrating both understanding and generation capabilities into one framework.
sentences.zh: 更实际的是，深圳可以直接生成RNA序列、计算机可读分子表示（SMILES）、全球气象领域和医学图像分割结果，有效地将理解和生成能力整合到一个框架中。

blocks.html_en: Open source and openness are key characteristics of this model. The Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence has released model weights, inference code, example scripts, and usage documentation. Researchers can download the model or call APIs through the Xinghe Qizhi Scientific Intelligence Open Platform, and use them together with over 1,500 scientific models and tools available on the platform; they can also download weights on Hugging Face and get code on GitHub, jointly building an open scientific intelligence community.

sentences.en: Open source and openness are key characteristics of this model.
sentences.zh: 开源和开放性是该模式的关键特征。

sentences.en: The Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence has released model weights, inference code, example scripts, and usage documentation.
sentences.zh: 上海科学情报研究院发布了模型权重、推理代码、示例脚本和使用文档。

sentences.en: Researchers can download the model or call APIs through the Xinghe Qizhi Scientific Intelligence Open Platform, and use them together with over 1,500 scientific models and tools available on the platform; they can also download weights on Hugging Face and get code on GitHub, jointly building an open scientific intelligence community.
sentences.zh: 研究人员可以通过星河启智科学情报开放平台下载模型或调用API，并与平台上1500多个科学模型和工具配合使用；他们还可以在Hugging Face上下载权重，在GitHub上获取代码，共同构建开放的科学情报社区。

blocks.html_en: This release took place on the stage of the Scientific Intelligence Open Forum at WAIC2026. The forum was guided by the Office of the World Artificial Intelligence Conference Organizing Committee, and hosted by Fudan University and the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence. Four distinguished scientists delivered keynote speeches, opening up different dimensions to understand scientific intelligence.

sentences.en: This release took place on the stage of the Scientific Intelligence Open Forum at WAIC2026.
sentences.zh: 本次发布是在 WAIC2026 科学情报开放论坛的舞台上进行的。

sentences.en: The forum was guided by the Office of the World Artificial Intelligence Conference Organizing Committee, and hosted by Fudan University and the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence.
sentences.zh: 本次论坛由世界人工智能大会组委会办公室指导，复旦大学、上海科学智能研究院主办。

sentences.en: It focused on the scientific closed-loop discovery in the AI-native era, gathering many renowned scientists and young representatives from industry, academia, and research, deeply discussing how artificial intelligence could restructure the entire process of scientific discovery from the methodology level, aiming to create a new ecological environment of open and collaborative scientific intelligence.
sentences.zh: 聚焦AI原生时代的科学闭环发现，云集众多产学研界知名科学家和青年代表，深入探讨人工智能如何从方法论层面重构科学发现全流程，旨在打造开放协作的科学智能新生态环境。

sentences.en: Four distinguished scientists delivered keynote speeches, opening up different dimensions to understand scientific intelligence.
sentences.zh: 四位杰出科学家发表主题演讲，开启理解科学智能的不同维度。

Wachter, winner of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry and professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen, emphasized that AI must be built on reliable physical mechanisms. Professor Gilles Brassard, winner of the Turing Award and professor at the University of Montreal, expressed high expectations for the potential of quantum computing in fields like new material discovery, and advised young researchers to follow their interests rather than industry trends, as current research findings might only truly take effect after a decade.

sentences.zh: 阿里·Y.教授

Wachter, winner of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry and professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen, emphasized that AI must be built on reliable physical mechanisms.
sentences.zh: 诺贝尔化学奖获得者、香港中文大学（深圳）教授沃赫特强调，人工智能必须建立在可靠的物理机制之上。

sentences.en: Professor Gilles Brassard, winner of the Turing Award and professor at the University of Montreal, expressed high expectations for the potential of quantum computing in fields like new material discovery, and advised young researchers to follow their interests rather than industry trends, as current research findings might only truly take effect after a decade.
sentences.zh: 图灵奖获得者、蒙特利尔大学教授 Gilles Brassard 教授对量子计算在新材料发现等领域的潜力表示了很高的期望，并建议年轻研究人员跟随自己的兴趣而不是行业趋势，因为当前的研究成果可能要在十年后才能真正生效。

sentences.en: Director of the Zhejiang Lab and academician of the Chinese Academy of Engineering, Wang Jian, shared his views on scientific foundation models and science as a whole.
sentences.zh: 之江实验室主任、中国工程院院士王健分享了他对科学基础模型和科学整体的看法。

sentences.en: He pointed out that scientific intelligence lies not in paper texts but in data, yet today's foundation models are still based on text.
sentences.zh: 他指出，科学智能不在于纸质文本，而在于数据，但今天的基础模型仍然基于文本。

sentences.en: In the future, scientific data should become the native inhabitants of scientific intelligence, meaning that data from various disciplines should be tokenized and included in a common representation space.
sentences.zh: 未来，科学数据应该成为科学智能的原生居民，这意味着来自不同学科的数据应该被标记化并包含在一个共同的表示空间中。

sentences.en: He believes AI is becoming as fundamental as mathematics, and the research paradigm will shift from STEM to STE+MAP, that is, the integration of mathematics, AI, and public infrastructure, making science a whole again.
sentences.zh: 他认为人工智能正在变得像数学一样基础，研究范式将从STEM转向STE+MAP，即数学、人工智能和公共基础设施的融合，使科学再次成为一个整体。

blocks.html_en: Professor Jianqing Fan, member of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences and professor at Princeton University, spoke on the topic of intelligent science and society, summarizing AI as a dynamic cycle of statistical learning and optimization decision-making, and explained how AI transforms data into social insights with examples such as socioeconomic measurement, financial risk control, and large model applications. He emphasized that AI serves socioeconomic development, while promoting intelligent agents and scientific innovation, it must continuously respond to issues such as changes in employment structure, educational transformation, ethical security, and whether humans can maintain effective control.

sentences.en: Professor Jianqing Fan, member of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences and professor at Princeton University, spoke on the topic of intelligent science and society, summarizing AI as a dynamic cycle of statistical learning and optimization decision-making, and explained how AI transforms data into social insights with examples such as socioeconomic measurement, financial risk control, and large model applications.
sentences.zh: 美国国家科学院院士、普林斯顿大学教授范建清教授以智能科学与社会为主题发表演讲，将人工智能概括为统计学习和优化决策的动态循环，并通过社会经济测量、金融风险控制、大模型应用等实例阐述了人工智能如何将数据转化为社会洞察。

sentences.en: He emphasized that AI serves socioeconomic development, while promoting intelligent agents and scientific innovation, it must continuously respond to issues such as changes in employment structure, educational transformation, ethical security, and whether humans can maintain effective control.
sentences.zh: 他强调，人工智能服务社会经济发展，在推动智能体和科学创新的同时，要不断应对就业结构变化、教育转型、伦理安全、人类能否保持有效控制等问题。

sentences.en: This requires not only systematic upgrades in research infrastructure, but also a fundamental transformation in the thinking patterns and capability structures of a generation of researchers.
sentences.zh: 这不仅需要科研基础设施的系统升级，更需要一代科研人员思维模式和能力结构的根本转变。

sentences.en: Qi Yuan, a specially appointed professor at Fudan University and director of the Shanghai Institute for Scientific Intelligence, outlined the ultimate vision of this transformation.
sentences.zh: 复旦大学特聘教授、上海科学情报研究所所长齐源概述了这一转变的最终愿景。

sentences.en: He stated that AI should move from predicting the next token to discovering unknown laws, and that the discovery of unknown scientific laws by AI will mark the beginning of super intelligence.
sentences.zh: 他表示，AI应该从预测下一个代币走向发现未知规律，而AI发现未知科学规律将标志着超级智能的开始。

sentences.en: The core of this process lies in compressing high-dimensional space to discover concise principles and achieving an efficient scientific verification loop, which will promote the integration of digital and physical worlds and the emergence of new model architectures.
sentences.zh: 这一过程的核心在于压缩高维空间以发现简洁原理并实现高效的科学验证循环，这将促进数字世界和物理世界的融合以及新模型架构的出现。

sentences.en: He reminded us that real research involves a long chain process including literature, hypotheses, data, models, simulations, experiments, and feedback, and the field of scientific intelligence is currently focusing on developing the systemic capabilities to organize complex research processes.
sentences.zh: 他提醒我们，真正的研究涉及文献、假设、数据、模型、模拟、实验和反馈等长链过程，而科学智能领域目前的重点是发展组织复杂研究过程的系统能力。

blocks.html_en: When Shenzhen, with its 11 billion parameters, brings six types of scientific data into one mind, the form of scientific discovery may be quietly rewritten.

sentences.en: When Shenzhen, with its 11 billion parameters, brings six types of scientific data into one mind, the form of scientific discovery may be quietly rewritten.
sentences.zh: 当拥有110亿个参数的深圳将六种科学数据集中到一个头脑中时，科学发现的形式可能会悄然改写。

content_html_zh:
7月19日，在2026世界人工智能大会科学智能开放论坛上，上海科学智能研究院发布了支撑多学科科学智能研究的基础超级大脑——深圳科学多模态基础模型。 名字来源于《西游记》中的“神珠铁”。 团队希望这种开放模型能够以相对简洁的形式开展多样化的科学任务，并让更多的研究人员参与测试、使用和共建。 它是今年3月初推出的“大圣”系统级智能研究代理，拥有超级大脑。

技术上，深圳以材料科学、生命科学、地球科学等多学科为基础，实现了跨领域科学知识和多模态综合理解的阶段性统一表征。 该模型拥有约 110 亿个参数，支持对六种类型的科学数据进行科学理解和多类型结果生成：DNA、RNA、蛋白质、小分子、地球系统和医学图像，所有这些都在一个模型中。 其共享主干采用Qwen3-VL-8B，六类科学数据中的每一种都有专用的数据处理路径。 在连接到共享模型之前，它保留了序列的序列顺序、分子连接结构、气象场空间分布以及医学图像的局部细节。 更实际的是，深圳可以直接生成RNA序列、计算机可读分子表示（SMILES）、全球气象领域和医学图像分割结果，有效地将理解和生成能力整合到一个框架中。

开源和开放性是该模式的关键特征。 上海科学情报研究院发布了模型权重、推理代码、示例脚本和使用文档。 研究人员可以通过星河启智科学情报开放平台下载模型或调用API，并与平台上1500多个科学模型和工具配合使用；他们还可以在Hugging Face上下载权重，在GitHub上获取代码，共同构建开放的科学情报社区。

本次发布是在 WAIC2026 科学情报开放论坛的舞台上进行的。 本次论坛由世界人工智能大会组委会办公室指导，复旦大学、上海科学智能研究院主办。 聚焦AI原生时代的科学闭环发现，云集众多产学研界知名科学家和青年代表，深入探讨人工智能如何从方法论层面重构科学发现全流程，旨在打造开放协作的科学智能新生态环境。 四位杰出科学家发表主题演讲，开启理解科学智能的不同维度。

阿里·Y.教授 K. 诺贝尔化学奖获得者、香港中文大学（深圳）教授沃赫特强调，人工智能必须建立在可靠的物理机制之上。 图灵奖获得者、蒙特利尔大学教授 Gilles Brassard 教授对量子计算在新材料发现等领域的潜力表示了很高的期望，并建议年轻研究人员跟随自己的兴趣而不是行业趋势，因为当前的研究成果可能要在十年后才能真正生效。

之江实验室主任、中国工程院院士王健分享了他对科学基础模型和科学整体的看法。 他指出，科学智能不在于纸质文本，而在于数据，但今天的基础模型仍然基于文本。 未来，科学数据应该成为科学智能的原生居民，这意味着来自不同学科的数据应该被标记化并包含在一个共同的表示空间中。 他认为人工智能正在变得像数学一样基础，研究范式将从STEM转向STE+MAP，即数学、人工智能和公共基础设施的融合，使科学再次成为一个整体。

美国国家科学院院士、普林斯顿大学教授范建清教授以智能科学与社会为主题发表演讲，将人工智能概括为统计学习和优化决策的动态循环，并通过社会经济测量、金融风险控制、大模型应用等实例阐述了人工智能如何将数据转化为社会洞察。 他强调，人工智能服务社会经济发展，在推动智能体和科学创新的同时，要不断应对就业结构变化、教育转型、伦理安全、人类能否保持有效控制等问题。

在峰会对话环节，与会嘉宾达成共识：AI原生时代原始创新的一大关键在于人、数据、模型、实验、学术判断能否形成新的协同发现机制。 这不仅需要科研基础设施的系统升级，更需要一代科研人员思维模式和能力结构的根本转变。

复旦大学特聘教授、上海科学情报研究所所长齐源概述了这一转变的最终愿景。 他表示，AI应该从预测下一个代币走向发现未知规律，而AI发现未知科学规律将标志着超级智能的开始。 这一过程的核心在于压缩高维空间以发现简洁原理并实现高效的科学验证循环，这将促进数字世界和物理世界的融合以及新模型架构的出现。 他提醒我们，真正的研究涉及文献、假设、数据、模型、模拟、实验和反馈等长链过程，而科学智能领域目前的重点是发展组织复杂研究过程的系统能力。

当拥有110亿个参数的深圳将六种科学数据集中到一个头脑中时，科学发现的形式可能会悄然改写。
